reasoning: The Lean definition of f(n) sums over Finset.Icc 0 n, which includes m=0. Since a_0 = 0/2 = 0 (0 is even), the extra term contributes nothing, so Lean's f(n) equals the informal f(n) = a_1 + ... + a_n for all n ≥ 0 (where the informal a_n uses 1-indexing to produce "0, 1, 1, 2, 2, 3, ..."). The integer divisions match since we evaluate at non-negative arguments (x+y > 0 and x-y > 0 under the hypotheses). The conclusion ∀ x y : ℤ, x > 0 ∧ y > 0 ∧ x > y → x*y = f(x+y) - f(x-y) precisely encodes "all positive integers x and y with x > y satisfy xy = f(x+y) - f(x-y)". Spot-check: x=3, y=2 gives xy=6 and f(5)-f(1)=6-0=6 ✓. The formalization is strictly faithful.
